sections.paragraph: m. (with Kametz impure), branches interwoven, a thicket, Gen 22:13. Here also as it seems to me belongs בִּסְבָךְ־עֵץ Psa 74:5, so that ( ָ) is long Kametz, although Metheg is wanting in the printed copies. A similar instance is מְנָת־חֶלְקִי Psa 16:5, where all the copies have the word without Metheg, although it is most certain that it should be read menâth; compare Psa 11:6 16:5 . A few copies have בִּסְבַךְ from סְבַךְ (of the form קְטַל), of which there is another trace in pl. סִבְכֵי הַיַּעַר Isa 9:17 10:34 .
